regionalBreakdown.excerpt: dollisia etenkin Etelä-Filippiinien Mindanaolla ja Metro-Manilassa. Seuraa säätietoja sadekaudella. Vältä kaikkea matkustamista Mindanaon lounaisosiin, Zamboangan niemimaalle ja Sulun saaristoon.

themes.heading: Poikkeava turvallisuustaso
themes.text: Mindanaon autonominen muslimialue: Vältä kaikkea matkustamista Zamboangan niemimaa: Vältä kaikkea matkustamista

themes.heading: Muut turvallisuustason tiedot
themes.text: Terrori-iskut ovat mahdollisia etenkin Etelä-Filippiinien Mindanaolla ja Metro-Manilassa. Seuraa säätietoja sadekaudella. Vältä kaikkea matkustamista Mindanaon lounaisosiin, Zamboangan niemimaalle ja Sulun saaristoon.

themes.heading: Ajankohtaista
themes.text: Ajankohtaista tietoa Filippiinien tilanteesta löydät edustuston verkkosivuilta ja seuraamalla paikallisviranomaisten tiedotusta. Edustusto päivittää ajankohtaista tietoa myös sosiaalisen median kanavilleen. UM:n matkustustiedotteet ovat suosituksia Vastaat matkapäätöksestäsi aina itse. Paikallisilla viranomaisilla on ensisijainen vastuu kaikkien maan alueella oleskelevien ihmisten turvallisuudesta ja hätätilanteissa etsintä- ja pelastustoimista. Kriisitilanteissa noudata paikallisten viranomaisten ohjeita. Seuraa uutisointia tiedotusvälineissä sekä tiedotusta edustuston verkkosivuilla ja sosiaalisen median kanavissa. Ulkoministeriö ja Suomen ulkomaanedustustot avustavat suomalaisia konsulipalvelulain mukaisesti. Mikäli Euroopan unionin ulkopuolisessa kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, voit kääntyä muiden EU-maiden edustustojen puoleen. Matkatoimiston järjestämällä matkalla olevaa avustaa ensisijaisesti matkanjärjestäjä. Konsulipalvelulaki Konsulisuojeludirektiivi Laki matkapalveluyhdistelmistä Suosittelemme aina matkavakuutuksen ottamista. Tarkista ennen matkaasi matkavakuutuksesi kattavuus ja voimassaolo erityisesti jos sinulla on jokin sairaus, olet raskaana, matkasi suuntautuu vaarallisille alueille tai harrastat riskialttiita lajeja, Sairastuneen tai vainajan kotiuttaminen on kallista ja vaikea järjestää ilman kattavaa matkavakuutusta, eikä hoitoa aina saa ulkomailla ilman maksusitoumusta tai ennakkomaksua. Tee matkustusilmoitus osoitteessa https://matkustusilmoitus.fi/

themes.heading: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne
themes.text: Ulkoministeriö suosittelee noudattamaan erityistä varovaisuutta ja tarkkaavaisuutta julkisilla paikoilla liikuttaessa. Terrori-iskut ja/tai poliittiset levottomuudet eivät ole poissuljettuja. Sisäpoliittinen tilanne on pääosin rauhallinen, mutta poliittiset mielenosoitukset ja väkijoukkojen kokoontumiset ovat mahdollisia. Rauhallisetkin mielenosoitukset voivat kasvaa kooltaan valtaviksi ja muuttua väkivaltaisiksi, väkijoukkojen kokoontumisia ei kannata mennä seuraamaan läheltä. Paikallisten juhlapäivien aikaan valmiustilaa nostetaan entisestään. Julkiset tilat kuten linja-autoterminaalit, lauttasatamat, ostoskeskukset ja kirkot saattavat olla iskujen kohteina. Turvallisuusjärjestelyt saattavat olla puutteellisia Manilan isoissa ostoskeskuksissa ja suuria väkijoukkoja houkuttelevissa tilaisuuksissa kuten konserteissa ja uskonnollisissa tapahtumissa. Filippiinien viranomaiset muistuttavat säännöllisesti terrori-iskujen mahdollisuudesta. Terrori-iskujen ja muiden levottomuuksien mahdollisuus keskittyy Mindanaolle ja Sulun saaristoon, mutta myös pääkaupunkiseutu Metro-Manila ja muut merkittävät kaupungit voivat olla mahdollisia iskukohteita. Keski-Visayasin saaristossa sekä Palawanin alueella, mukaan lukien Puerto Princesa City ja Puerto Princesa Subterranean River National Park, on olemassa pieni kidnappausriski ja terroriuhka. Aseellisia välikohtauksia esiintyy Filippiinien armeijan ja muslimiseparatistien välillä Mindanaolla sekä kommunistisen ääriliikkeen alueilla muun muassa Pohjois-Luzonilla ja Itä-Mindanaolla. Koko Mindanaon saarella on ollut voimassa poikkeustilalaki (martial law) terrorisminvastaisten operaatioiden vuoksi. Vaikka poikkeustilalaki päättyi vuoden 2019 lopussa, ei Mindanaon saarella ole päästy täysin normaalioloihin. Mindanaolle matkustavien on syytä seurata tilannetta erityisen tarkasti paikallisista tiedotusvälineistä ja noudattaa paikallisviranomaisten määräyksiä. Siviiliuhreja vaatineita yhteenottoja on tapahtunut myös Visayasin saaristossa Boholilla, osassa Keski-Mindanaota esimerkiksi Pohjois-Cotabaton, Lanao del Surin ja Lanao del Norten maakunnissa. Pienimuotoisia pommi- ja kranaatti-iskuja on tapahtunut Mindanaon muslimialueiden (BARMM Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao) lisäksi muun muassa Cotabaton, Kidapawan, Zamboangan, General Santosin, Davaon, Illiganin ja Cagayan de Oron alueilla. Terroristijärjestö Abu Sayyafin ja armeijan väliset yhteenotot jatkuvat Mindanaolla ja erityisesti Sulun saaristossa, mukaan lukien Basilanin, Tawi Tawin ja Jolon saarilla. Samoilla alueilla on viime vuosien aikana tapahtunut myös joitakin länsimaalaisten kidnappaustapatuksia. Levottomuuksien, aseellisten yhteenottojen mahdollisuuden ja sieppausuhan vuoksi tulisi välttää matkustamista Mindanaon lounaisosiin, Zamboangan niemimaalle ja Sulun saaristoon. Matkailua eristyksissä oleviin Etelä-Filippiinien rantakohteisiin ei suositella läheisillä merialueilla operoivien sieppausryhmien vuoksi. Sieppausten kohteena ovat useimmiten hallituksen edustajat tai varakkaat paikalliset, mutta matkailijan kannattaa silti noudattaa varovaisuutta. Yleinen hätänumero on 911.

themes.heading: Rikollisuus
themes.text: Köyhyydestä johtuva katurikollisuus on Filippiineillä yleistä. Muun muassa käsilaukkujen ryöstöt ovat tavanomaisia ja ne ovat johtaneet jopa henkirikoksiin. Ryöstäjät käyttävät usein moottoripyörää. Aika ajoin varoitetaan ostoskeskuksissa ja yökerhoissa yksin liikkuvia ryöstöistä, joissa voidaan käyttää lamaannuttavia kemikaaleja. Pimeitä teitä ja väentungoksia kannattaa välttää, samoin kuin yksin matkustamista Metro-Manilan ulkopuolella ja öiseen aikaan myös kaupunkialueella. Taksikuskien tiedetään osallistuneen ryöstöihin. Taksi on suositeltavaa ottaa valvotulta alueelta kuten ostoskeskusten ja hotellien virallisilta taksitolpilta, jolloin taksin rekisterinumero kirjataan ylös. Nettitilauksena paikalle saatava Grab on suosittu, sillä matkan hinta määräytyy jo tilattaessa. Vieraiden tarjoamiin juomiin ja ruokiin on syytä suhtautua varauksellisesti. Niihin on voitu lisätä tyrmäystippoja ryöstö- tai päällekarkausaikomuksissa. Poliisin ja rikollisten välisten yhteenottojen ja ammuskelujen harhaluodit voivat vaatia myös ulkopuolisia uhreja. Näissä tilanteissa kannattaa poistua paikalta mahdollisimman nopeasti. Huumausainerikoksiin suhtaudutaan erittäin ankarasti. Henkilöllisyys on pystyttävä pyydettäessä todistamaan poliisille; tähän riittää yleensä passikopio. Jos passisi katoaa tai varastetaan, tee siitä ilmoitus paikalliselle poliisiasemalle. Mikäli kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, hae hätämatkustusasiakirjaa (ETD = Emergency Travel Document) toisen EU-maan edustustosta. Ota kopiot tärkeimmistä asiapapereista kuten passista ja lentolipuista. Säilytä ne erillään alkuperäisistä.

themes.heading: Liikenne
themes.text: Liikennekulttuuri poikkeaa huomattavasti suomalaisesta. Kulkuvälineet voivat olla heikkokuntoisia, ja ajotyyli on ajoittain aggressiivista ja ennakoimatonta. Teiden kunto voi olla kaupungeissa ja erityisesti maaseudulla heikkoa. Yleensä filippiiniläisissä autoissa ei ole vakuutuksia, mikä on onnettomuuden sattuessa hyvä tietää. Maan linja-autokanta on osittain heikkoa ja vanhaa. Kuolemiin johtaneita turmia tapahtuu pitkillä ajomatkoilla aika ajoin. Kehotamme noudattamaan harkintaa linja-autoyhtiön valinnassa. Yöaikaan kulkevat linjat eivät ole suositeltavia. Etenkin Metro-Manilassa liikenneruuhkat voivat viivästyttää matkantekoa merkittävästi. Armeijan ja poliisin tehostetut turvatarkastukset Metro-Manilan alueella sekä keskeisiksi luokitelluissa kohteissa, kuten lentokentillä, satamissa ja pääliikenneväylillä aiheuttavat viivästymisiä. Matkailijan kannattaa varautua autojen ja käsilaukkujen tarkastuksiin saavuttaessa julkisiin tiloihin, kuten ostoskeskuksiin ja hotelleihin. Liikuntaesteisille Filippiinit on haasteellinen. Jalkakäytävät puuttuvat usein kokonaan, eikä omaehtoinen liikkuminen ole mahdollista. Kuljettajan ja henkilökohtaisen avustajan palkkaaminen on suhteellisen helppoa. Laivaonnettomuuksia tapahtuu ajoittain, koska alukset ovat huonokuntoisia ja usein ylikuormitettuja, eikä turvamääräyksiä noudateta.

themes.heading: Luonnonolot
themes.text: Filippiinit kuuluu niin sanottuun Tyynenmeren tulirengas -alueeseen, joka on altis luonnonkatastrofeille, kuten maanjäristyksille, tulivuorenpurkauksille ja pyörremyrskyille. Merenalaiset maanjäristykset voivat aiheuttaa myös hyökyaaltoja. Filippiineillä sijaitsee 22 aktiiviseksi luokiteltua tulivuorta. Filippiinejä koettelevat vuosittain useat pyörremyrskyt, joista pyritään varoittamaan etukäteen tiedotusvälineissä. Varsinainen sadekausi on kesäkuusta marraskuuhun, mutta pyörremyrskyjä voi esiintyä myös sadekauden ulkopuolella. Hirmumyrskyjen yhteydessä kannattaa ottaa huomioon myös tulva- ja maanvyörymävaarat, jotka voivat aiheuttaa suuriakin tuhoja. Hirmumyrskyn iskiessä, seurauksena voi olla välittömiä vaikutuksia infrastruktuuriin, sähkönjakeluun ja verkkoyhteyksiin. Elintarvikkeista, vedestä ja käteisvaluutasta voi olla puutetta. Erilaisiin luonnonmullistuksiin kannattaa varautua mahdollisuuksien mukaan etukäteen. Niiden aikana liikkuminen käy hankalaksi, jopa vaaralliseksi, ja paikoittain kulku- ja puhelinyhteydet voivat katketa kokonaan. Varoitukset ja viranomaisohjeet voivat tulla lyhyellä ennakointiajalla, joten paikallistiedotteita tulee seurata erityisen tarkkaavaisesti. Lisätietoja: Filippiinien meteorologian laitos, PAGASA (englanniksi) Filippiinien vulkanologian ja seismologian laitos, PHIVOLCS (englanniksi) Filippiinien katastrofikeskus, NDRRMC (englanniksi) Ohjeita maanjäristystilanteeseen, Helsingin yliopiston seismologian laitos Tyynenmeren tsunamikeskus, PTWC (englanniksi) Kansainvälinen tsunamikeskus International Tsunami Information Center (englanniksi) Maailmanlaajuinen maanjäristystietokanta, USGS (englanniksi)

themes.heading: Terveys
themes.text: Kaiken kattava matkustajavakuutus on tarpeen. Kun saapuu maahan ja poistuu maasta on täytettävä eTravel Pass jossa kysytään myös rokotuksista. Kesäkuusta marraskuuhun kestävällä sadekaudella Filippiinien terveysviranomaiset ovat antaneet varoituksia kulkutautien kasvavasta riskistä. Huolellisella hygienialla riskejä voi kuitenkin pienentää merkittävästi. Kuuma ja kostea ilmasto voi aiheuttaa rasitusta joillekin matkailijoille. Vesijohtoveden laadun vuoksi on turvallisinta juoda pullotettua vettä. Filippiineillä esiintyy hyttysten levittämää dengue- ja chikungunyakuumetta sekä zikavirusta. Myös malariaa esiintyy alueittain. Ainoa keino ehkäistä hyttysvälitteisen taudin tarttumista on suojautua hyttysten pistoilta käyttämällä karkotteita sekä pukeutumalla pitkähihaisiin ja -lahkeisiin vaaleisiin vaatteisiin. Erityisen tärkeää suojautuminen on raskaana oleville. Myös rabiesta eli vesikauhua esiintyy eri puolilla Filippiinejä. Kontaktia eläimiin, erityisesti koiriin ja kissoihin, kannattaa välttää. Filippiinien terveysministeriö (englanniksi) Selvitä ennakkoon matkakohteesi tartuntatautitilanne ja rokotussuositukset. Löydät ne seuraavien viranomaisten verkkosivuilta: Matkailijan terveysopas Maailman terveysjärjestö WHO Euroopan tartuntatautivirasto

themes.heading: Maan lait ja käytännöt
themes.text: Matkailija on velvollinen noudattamaan paikallista lainsäädäntöä. Tuomiokäytäntö esimerkiksi huumausainerikoksissa on eurooppalaisittain ajatellen ankara. Myös pienen huumemäärän hallussapidosta voidaan tuomita vankeutta. Lasten seksuaalinen hyväksikäyttö on Filippiineillä rangaistava teko, jonka tuomiona on yleensä vankeutta.

themes.heading: Maahantulosäännökset
themes.text: Matkustusrajoitukset voivat muuttua nopeasti, joten on tärkeää seurata ajantasaisia viranomaistiedotteita, suurlähetystön verkkosivuja ja sosiaalista mediaa. Lisätietoja: Filippiinien maahanmuuttoviranomainen (englanniksi) Filippiinien tullilaitos (englanniksi) Filippiinien suurlähetystö Tukholmassa Filippiinien suurlähetystö Oslossa (englanniksi) UM ei vastaa vieraiden valtioiden maahantulosäännöksistä, kuten passin voimassaoloa koskevista ehdoista tai viisumivelvollisuudesta. Varmista ajantasaiset maahantulosäädökset kohdemaan viranomaisilta, esimerkiksi maan lähimmästä edustustosta. Lue lisää Suomen kansalaisen matkustusasiakirjoista osoitteessa https://um.fi/passi/

themes.heading: Sicherheitsstufe
themes.text: country-security"> Stand 23.07.2026 (Unverändert gültig seit: 31.03.2026) Sicherheitsstufe Sicherheitsstufe 4 (regional) seit 01.01.2014 Landkarte Regionale Reisewarnung Sicherheitsstufe 4 (von 4) gilt auf der gesamten Westküste der Insel Mindanao inklusive der Bangsamoro Autonomous Region (BARMM) und dem Sulu Archipel (zwischen Mindanao und Malaysia). Es wird vor Segel- und Tauchrouten in der Süd-Sulu-See gewarnt. In diesen Gebieten sind Terrorgruppen aktiv. Regionale Reisewarnung Sicherheitsstufe 3 (von 4) gilt für den restlichen Teil der Insel Mindanao. Sicherheitsrisiko Sicherheitsstufe 2 (von 4) gilt im Rest des Landes. Die Sicherheitslage kann sich jederzeit verändern und entwickeln. Daher empfehlen wir Ihnen: Nützen Sie unsere Auslandsservice-App sowie die kostenlose Reiseregistrierung! Informieren Sie sich über die Sicherheitslage vor Ort und überprüfen Sie diese regelmäßig! <div

themes.heading: Aktuelle Hinweise
themes.text: Taifun-Warnung! Die philippinische Wetterbehörde PAGASA http://www.pagasa.dost.gov.ph/ bietet Informationen in englischer Sprache über aktuelle Wetterwarnungen. Nehmen Sie Evakuierungsaufforderungen in besonders gefährdeten Gebieten ernst und befolgen Sie Anweisungen lokaler Behörden. Verfolgen Sie Wettervorhersagen aufmerksam.

themes.heading: Sicherheit & Kriminalität
themes.text: Regionale Reisewarnung (Sicherheitsstufe 4) für ein bestimmtes Gebiet! Vor Reisen in den Westen der Insel Mindanao (Provinzen Basilan, Lanao del Sur, Maguindanoa, Sulu, Tawi-Tawi) und dem Sulu Archipel (zwischen Mindanao und Malaysia) sowie vor Segel- und Tauchrouten in der Süd-Sulu-See wird gewarnt. In diesen Gebieten sind Terrorgruppen aktiv. Es kommt immer wieder zu Kampfhandlungen mit der Armee und anderen Einsatzkräften, Bombenattentaten auf zivile Ziele, Entführungen von Ausländern sowie zu Überfällen auf Segel- und Tauchboote und Strandhotels. Hinweis: Reisewarnungen werden im Regelfall nur in besonderen Krisensituationen ausgesprochen, wenn eine generelle Gefährdung für Leib und Leben besteht. Reisende, die sich in ein Gebiet mit Reisewarnung begeben, müssen sich der möglichen Konsequenzen bewusst sein: Konsularische Hilfeleistung für in Not geratene Österreicherinnen und Österreicher ist nur äußerst eingeschränkt möglich. Bei Reisen in Gebiete mit Reisewarnung können Versicherungen trotz eines aufrechten Versicherungsvertrages Ausschlussgründe geltend machen und sind damit leistungsfrei. Das Konsulargebührengesetz ermächtigt die Republik Österreich, die Kosten für allenfalls erforderliche Schutzmaßnahmen bzw. Hilfeleistung in bestimmten Situationen im Regressweg von Reisenden zurückzufordern! Die Gefahr von Terroranschlägen ist landesweit gegeben, wobei auch allgemeine Kriminalität, wie bewaffnete Überfalle, Kindesentführungen, etc. nicht ausgeschlossen werden kann. Sicherheitsrisiko (Sicherheitsstufe 3) für den Rest der Insel Mindanao Sicherheitsrisiko (Sicherheitsstufe 2) für den Rest des Landes. Es besteht eine grundsätzliche Gefahr für terroristische Anschläge zu Land und zur See. Anweisungen von Sicherheitskräften ist Folge zu leisten. Überlandreisen sollten nur in Gruppen und mit seriösen Reiseveranstaltern unternommen werden. Der Aufenthalt in sichtbar verarmten und verwahrlosten Stadtteilen, auch in der Hauptstadt Manila, ist zu vermeiden. Es wird geraten, sich in der Nacht nur in Gruppen zu bewegen. Allgemeine Notrufnummern vor Ort Hauptnotfallsnummer: 911 Rettung: 143 Feuerwehr(national): +63 2 8426-3812 +63 2 8426-0231 +63 2 8426-0219 Polizei PNP Philippine National Police: +63 2 8723-0401 +63 2 8537-4500 Zivilschutz: +63 2 8911 5061 +63 2 8421 1918 Katastrophenschutz: National Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (NDRRMC) +63 2 8912 2665 +63 2 8911 1406 NCR Operation Center Mobile: +63 919 0025470 Küstenwache: Command Center Hotline: (+63/2) 8527-3877 Mobile: (+63) 9777928382, (+63) 9662179610 Vulkan und Erdbebeninstitut (PHIVOLCS): (+63/2) 8426-1468; 8426-1469; 8426-1470 Touristenministerium: Hotline: 151-8687 Mobile: (+63)954 2533215

themes.heading: Einreise & Ausreise
themes.text: Reiseregistrierung: Vor der Einreise und auch vor der beabsichtigten Ausreise ist eine Registrierung über das eTravel Portal der philippinischen Regierung verpflichtend erforderlich. Der Nachweis der Registrierung in Form eines QR-Codes oder Barcodes wird von Fluggesellschaften am Check-in häufig angefordert und muss auch bei der Ein- bzw. Ausreise vorgewiesen werden. Die Registrierung kann frühestens 72 Stunden vor der Ein- bzw. Ausreise durchgeführt werden. Visumpflicht: Nein, jedoch nur für einen Aufenthalt von maximal dreißig Tagen ist kein Visum erforderlich. Reisende müssen im Besitz eines gültigen Rückflugtickets in ihr Herkunftsland oder ihr nächstes Zielland sowie eines Reisepasses sein, der noch mindestens sechs Monate über die Dauer des Aufenthalts auf den Philippinen hinaus gültig ist. Visum erhältlich: Bei einem längerem Aufenthalt ist vor der Reise eine Antragstellung bei der Botschaft der Republik der Philippinen bzw. ab 29. September 2025 über das e-Visa System (https://evisa.gov.ph) möglich Reisedokumente: Gültiger Reisepass Passgültigkeit: Zumindest 6 Monate bei Ausreise eAusweise: Elektronische Ausweise (ID Austria) werden derzeit ausschließlich in Österreich akzeptiert. Cremefarbiger Notpass: Wird akzeptiert, der Notpass muss bei Einreise zumindest noch 6 Monate gültig sein. „Visa on Arrival" für max. 30 Tage. Sonstiges: Reisende müssen ihre Wiederausreiseabsicht durch Vorlage eines Rückflug- oder Weiterreisetickets sowie genügend Geldmittel für den Aufenthalt nachweisen können. Die kostenpflichtige Verlängerung des Einreisevisums bis maximal 59 Tage ist beim Bureau of Immigration (Magallanes Drive, Intramuros, Manila, Philippines 1002) oder in einem der zahlreichen Regionalbüros des Bureau of Immigration unter Vorlage eines gültigen Rückflugtickets möglich. Bei illegalem Aufenthalt wird die Ausreise erst nach Bezahlung hoher Strafgebühren oder Haft gestattet. Die Bezahlung muss beim Bureau of Immigration in Intramuros erfolgen. Für aktuelle rechtsgültige Einreisevorschriften auf den Philippinen, beachten Sie die Informationen der zuständigen philippinischen Behörden, dem Bureau of Immigration, Department of Health und der Philippinische Botschaft in Wien. Zusammenfassung der entsprechenden philippinischen Visakategorien Minderjährige: Für Minderjährige, die alleine oder mit einer nicht erziehungsberechtigten Person auf die Philippinen reisen, sind zusätzliche Anforderungen zu erfüllen: 1. Wenn sie von einer anderen Person als die Erziehungsberechtigten begleitet werden, muss bei einer philippinischen Botschaft eine „Authorization to Accompany Child“ beantragt werden. 2. Österreichische Minderjährige müssen vor der Reise bei der zuständigen philippinischen Botschaft den Waiver for Exclusion Ground (WEG) beantragen. Die Anforderungen finden Sie hier: Waiver for Exclusion Ground | Bureau of Immigration Philippines. Bei der Ausreise sind keine weiteren Voraussetzungen zu erfüllen. 3. Ausländische minderjährige Kinder, die ihren ausländischen Pass zum ersten Mal verwenden sollen (z.B. auf den Philippinen geborene Österreicher), benötigen eine Ausreisegenehmigung (Emigration Exit Clearance (EEC)), die bei den philippinischen Immigrationsbehörden beantragt werden muss. Information finden Sie hier: FAQ's | Bureau of Immigration Philippines. Diebstahl/Verlust des Reisedokuments: Von der Verwendung gestohlener oder verlorener und wieder gefundener Reisedokumente wird abgeraten, auch wenn die Anzeige bei der zuständigen Behörde bereits widerrufen wurde, da Probleme an der Grenze bis zur Einreiseverweigerung nicht ausgeschlossen werden können. Konventions- oder Fremdenpass: Wenden Sie sich bitte an die weiter unten angeführte „ausländische Vertretung in Österreich“.

themes.heading: Einfuhr & Ausfuhr
themes.text: Die Ein- und Ausfuhr von bis zu 50.000 PHP und 10.000 USD ist gestattet. Euro können lokal getauscht werden, es empfiehlt sich auch die Mitnahme von Kreditkarten oder US-Dollar in bar. Die Einlösung von Travellerschecks ist oft schwierig; entsprechende Quittungen der ausstellenden Geldinstitute müssen bei der Einlösung vorgewiesen werden. Importverbote bestehen für Nachtsichtgeräte, Waffen, Waffenteile und Munition, auch wenn sie nur dekorativen Charakter haben. Nähere Auskünfte finden Sie auch im Travel Centre der IATA. Die angeführten Mengen und Beträge sind unverbindliche Richtangaben, rechtsverbindliche Informationen kann nur die Vertretungsbehörde dieses Landes erteilen. Bitte beachten Sie bei der Einreise nach Österreich die geltenden Einfuhrbestimmungen.

themes.heading: Gesundheit
themes.text: Krankenhäuser entsprechen nicht dem europäischen Standard, in Manila teilweise. Während der Regenzeit ist aufgrund von Dengue-Infektionen besondere Vorsicht geboten. Nähere Informationen: Department of Health, IAMAT, Inquirer News Nehmen Sie Insektenschutz zur Vermeidung von Tropenkrankheiten mit. Ausführliche Informationen zu gängigen Infektionskrankheiten auf Reisen erhalten Sie auch beim Öffentlichen Gesundheitsportal Österreichs bzw. bei den tropenmedizinischen Instituten. Saisonal kommt es zum sogenannten Phänomen „red tide“ oder Algenblüte aktuellen Aussendung. Das philippinische Landwirtschaftsministerium warnt hier vor dem Verzehr von Meeresfrüchten in südlichen Provinzen der Philippinen. Es besteht kein Sozialversicherungsabkommen mit Österreich. Es wird darauf hingewiesen, dass die Kosten einer allfälligen Krankenhausbehandlung erheblich sein können. Der Abschluss einer alle Risiken abdeckenden Reiseversicherung wird daher vor Reiseantritt dringend empfohlen. Die Mitnahme einer Reiseapotheke, die nicht nur regelmäßig benötigte Arzneimittel, sondern auch Medikamente für gängige Reiseerkrankungen beinhaltet, wird empfohlen. Wer auf bestimmte Medikamente angewiesen ist, sollte einen ausreichenden Vorrat und einen Nachweis über die ärztliche Verschreibung mitnehmen, auf der Homepage des Sozialministeriums finden Sie nähere Informationen zur Mitnahme von Medikamenten ins Ausland. Es wird empfohlen, rechtzeitig vor Reisebeginn den Hausarzt oder eine andere geeignete Einrichtung zu kontaktieren, um sich über eventuell erforderliche Reiseimpfungen zu erkundigen.

themes.heading: Infrastruktur
themes.text: Öffentliche Verkehrsverbindungen: Von der Benutzung öffentlicher Verkehrsmittel wird abgeraten. Taxis und Mietwagen sind vorhanden. Bei Taxifahrten soll der Taxameter verwendet oder der Fahrpreis vor Antritt der Fahrt vereinbart werden. Gefahr von Raubüberfällen bei Verwendung von Privatfahrzeugen oder illegale Taxidienste. Vorsicht vorTaxi-, Tricycle- oder Calesafahrern, die zu sich nach Hause einladen oder zu Karten- bzw. Würfelspielen auffordern! Der österreichischen Führerschein ist zusätzlich mit einen internationalen Führerschein gültig. Mehr Informationen zur Straßenverkehrsordnung finden Sie in der Länderdatenbank des ÖAMTC.

themes.heading: Umwelt
themes.text: Wechsel von Regenzeit (Juni bis November) mit hoher Luftfeuchtigkeit und häufigen Taifunen und Trockenzeit (Dezember bis Februar) sowie anschließender heiße Abschnitt der Trockenzeit von März bis Mai. Jährlich 20–30 Taifunen, die zahlreiche Menschenleben fordern und teilweise verheerende Schäden anrichten. Informieren Sie sich in der Taifunsaison über die Großwetterlage, gleiches gilt für Bootsfahren! Aktuelle Meldungen können auf der Website des Wetterdienstes Pagasa abgerufen werden. Erdbebenaktive Zone: Informationen finden Sie hier - : Regionale Hinweise, Nationales US-Geologieinstitut, Österreichischer Zivilschutzverband (mit Erdbebenschutz-Ratgeber)

themes.heading: Besondere Bestimmungen
themes.text: Der Besitz geringer Drogenmengen ist strafbar und führt zu hohen Freiheitsstrafen (ab 5g lebenslang Freiheitsstrafe) Ab 18 Jahren ist Homosexualität legal. Hohe Freiheitsstrafen bei Sexualdelikten und insbesondere bei Kindesmissbrauch; unklare Abgrenzung zu Prostitution, die verboten ist. Das Zusammenleben unverheirateter Paare ist nicht verboten, wird aber gesellschaftlich strikt abgelehnt. Das Schutzalter für sexuelle Handlungen kann gegenüber den in Österreich geltenden Bestimmungen um einige Jahre höher sein oder sogar über dem Erwachsenenalter von 18 Jahren liegen. Es können jedoch auch (beispielsweise in einzelnen Provinzen oder Regionen) unterschiedliche Bestimmungen zur Anwendung kommen. Bitte informieren Sie sich rechtzeitig bei der Vertretungsbehörde dieses Landes.

themes.text: ㅇ 위치: 마닐라 기준 북위 13도, 동경 122도 ㅇ 수도 : 마닐라 ㅇ 면적 : 약 300,000 ㎢ (한반도의 1.3배) - 7,107개의 도서로 구성, 전체의 65%가 산악지대, 루손(104,688㎢)과 민다나오 (101,999㎢)가 총면적의 65%차지 ㅇ 언어 : 영어, 따갈로그어 (공용) ㅇ 인종 :말레이계가 주종이며 중국, 미국, 스페인계 혼혈 다수 ㅇ 종교 : 가톨릭 83 %, 개신교 9%, 이슬람교 5% 불교 및 기타 3%

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ※ 필리핀은 살인ㆍ납치ㆍ강도 등 강력사건이 빈번히 발생하고 있으므로 안전에 유의하시기 바랍니다. □ 살인 사건의 경우 ㅇ 최근 3년간 (2023-2025) 우리 국민이 연루된 피살 사건은 총 5건으로, 대부분 불법 총기 강도로 확인됨. ('23년: 1건, '24년: 2건, '25년: 2건) ㅇ 생계형 강도에 따른 사망이 대부분이며, 치정 문제, 현지 여성관의 관계중 발생한 갈등 등 복합적인 형태로 나타 나고 있음. ㅇ 살인 사건의 유형은 다양하지만, 대표적인 경우로는 - 카지노 및 유흥 목적의 단기 방문 중, 금전거래에 얽힌 현지인에 의해 금품 탈취 및 살해 - 연애ㆍ치정 관련된 연적이나 동거인에 의한 폭력 및 살인 - 해고된 현지 직원들의 보복 범죄 (운전기사, 가정부 등) - 무장강도 후 살해 (총기 또는 흉기 사용) □ 납치ㆍ감금 사건의 경우 ㅇ 은퇴 또는 장기 체류 중이던 국민이 거주지 인근에서 납치 ㅇ 카지노 조직 또는 채무 갈등으로 인한 감금 (자금 송금ㆍ지불을 요구) ㅇ 고소 협박을 동반한 감금 형태 (성범죄 등 누명) ㅇ 공항 또는 호텔 등에서 현지인과 짜고 납치 후 금품 요구 □ 강절도 사건의 경우 ㅇ 최근 3년간 총기 또는 오토바이 2인조 강도 사건 다수 발생 -특히 말라떼, 마카티, 파사이, 앙헬레스 등 외국인 밀집 지역에서 빈번하게 보고됨 ㅇ 주요 수법으로는 - 오토바이 2인조 무장강도가 외국인을 대상으로 총기등으로 위협 후 가방, 휴대폰, 현금 등을 탈취 - 약물강도: 클럽ㆍ음식점 등에서 음료에 약물 혼합 후 의식 잃게 해 금품 탈취 - 무장 경찰 가장: 검문을 방지해 마약 또는 총기ㆍ마약을 몰래 넣고 체포 후 금품 요구 - 공모사건: 여성과 공모해 성범죄 누명을 씌운 뒤 돈 요구

themes.heading: 유의해야할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 민다나오 지역은 이슬람 반군 및 공산 반군 세력이 활동하고 있어 외국인 납치 위험 상존 - 잠보앙가, 술루, 바실란 지역은 절대 방문 자제 권고 ㅇ 메트로 마닐라 및 앙헬레스, 바탕가스, 세부 등 외국인 밀집 지역 - 심야 시간대 강도, 납치, 절도, 약물범죄 빈번 발생 ㅇ 카지노 밀집 지역 (마닐라, 앙헬레스) - 고리대금, 감금, 사기, 폭력사건 빈번 ㅇ 세부지역 - 다이빙, 제트스키 등 물놀이 사고 다수 발생 - 안전 수칙 미준수로 인한 사망 사고 주의

themes.heading: 자연재해
themes.text: ㅇ 6~10월 우기에는 연간 20개 이상의 태풍이 발생, 루손ㆍ비사야 지역에 집중 - 강풍, 정전, 산사태, 도로 침수 등 피해 유의 ㅇ 지진 및 화산 활동 - 2024년 따알(Taal), 마욘(Mayon), 칸라온(Kanlaon) 화산 분화 경고 발생 - 칸라온 화산의 경우 2025년 5월 현재 화산경보 3단계 - 필리핀 전역에서 규모 4.0 이상의 지진이 월 평균 45회 정도 발생함 - 여행 중 기상예보 및 화산경보 수시 확인 필수

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : O, (무기한) (협정) ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : O, (무기한) (협정) ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : O, (30일) (일방) ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : 도착사증 발급(30일까지 체류 가능하며 최대 3년까지 연장 가능) 입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 입국 72시간 전까지 eTravel 등록(https://etravel.gov.ph) ㅇ 입출국시 페소의 동전, 화폐, 수표, 기타 유가증권 금액이 5만페소, 미화 1만불까지 소지가능하며, 기준 금액을 초과하는 경우는 반드시 필리핀 중앙은행(BSP)에 전체 금액을 신고해야 함. 사전 신고없이 반입 또는 반출시 초과액 압수 및 벌금, 행정조치, 형사처분을 받을 수 있음. 외환신고서 입국전 온라인 신고.제출 또는 필리핀공항 세관에서 작성 후 제출 가능 - 온라인사이트 : https://ideclar.customs.gov.ph ㅇ 판매 목적의 명품 모조품, 가품 반입 시 압수 또는 몰수될 수 있음 출국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 출국 72시간 전까지 eTravel 등록(https://etravel.gov.ph) 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 : www.philembassy-seoul.com ㅇ 해당국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : www.overseas.mofa.go.kr/ph-ko/index.do ㅇ 기타 참고자료 - 필리핀 외교부 : www.dfa.gov.ph - 필리핀 이민청 : www.immigration.gov.ph - 필리핀 곤광부 한국사무소 : www.itsmorefuninthephilippines.co.kr

themes.heading: 일반문화
themes.text: ㅇ 필리핀에서는 인사를 할 때 친척간에는 주로 어른의 손을 아랫사람이 자신의 이마에 대며(mano), 대체적으로는 악수를 나누고 사교적인 곳에서는 뺨을 맞대는 경우도 자주 있음 ㅇ 필리핀 국민은 자신이 옳다고 생각하는 일을 하기보다는 남들이 어떻게 생각하는 지에 더 신경을 쓰는 경향이 있으며, hiya(히야; 체면, 부끄러움)를 매우 중시합니다. - 현지인을 유순하고 쉬운 상대로 착각하고, 모욕을 주거나 공개적으로 부정적인 감정을 강하게 드러낼 경우, 불의의 사고를 당할 수 있으므로, 필리핀 여행시 현지인의 감정을 자극하는 언동이나 행동은 반드시 삼가야 합니다. ㅇ 필리핀에는 도로사정이 좋지 않아 항상 교통체증이 심합니다. 따라서 약속 시간보다 30분 내지 한 시간 정도 늦더라도 크게 실례라고 생각하지 않습니다. ㅇ 필리핀 사람들은 식사 초대에 사전양해 없이 다른 사람(부하, 친구, 비서 등)을 동반하는 경우가 있으니, 항상 여유있게 예약하고 비용을 넉넉하게 준비하는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 종교관련
themes.text: ㅇ 필리핀 사람들은 종교가 일상 생활화 되어 있으며, 마을 축제는 물론 국가 지정 공휴일도 가톨릭 전통과 연결이 되어 있습니다. 소수이지만 이슬람교의 주요 행사날을 공휴일로 지정하기도 합니다. ㅇ 가톨릭의 규율에 따라 낙태가 철저히 금지되어 있고, 이혼도 결혼 무효를 제외하고는 인정하지 않고 있습니다.

themes.heading: 팁 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 서비스를 받을 때는 팁을 주는 것이 관례로서, 보통 50 ~ 100페소 내외를 팁으로 줍니다.

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 한국과 운전석 위치가 같음 ㅇ 마닐라 지역에서는 차량 5부제 정책 시행 ㅇ 국제 면허증은 입국일로부터 90일까지만 허용 ㅇ 운전시 면허증, 여권(사본), 차량 등록증 사본 및 보험 증명서 소지 필요 ㅇ 임대차량 운전자는 최소 21세 이상이여야 함 ㅇ 운전 가능 최소 연령은 student license를 소지한 16세 이상의 운전자임, 다만, 운전면허증 소지자가 조수석에 탑승해야 함

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ 택시 약물강도단(Ativan Gang) 사고: 택시 운전자가 사탕이나 음료수 등에 강력한 수면제를 넣어 정신을 잃게 한 후 금품을 털어가는 범죄 유형 ㅇ 택시 소매치기: 택시를 잡고 탑승하려고 하는 순간 불상인이 나타나 본인이 먼저 택시를 잡았다고 실랑이를 할 때 핸드폰 등 귀중품을 강탈

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생시 911로 경찰에 신고 ㅇ 경미한 접촉사고는 상대방 운전자와 현장에서 각자의 파손 부분을 수리하는 정도로 절충하여 합의할 수 있음. 합의한 내용을 문서(합의서) 또는 녹음으로 증비자료 확보하는게 중요함 ㅇ 교통사고 발생시 상대방과 지나친 언쟁 삼가, 이는 단순 말다툼이 큰 싸움으로 번져 총격으로 이어지는 사례 발생 [대중교통] ㅇ 버스 및 소형택시(흰색, Ponto de Taxi)가 있으나 버스 내에서 강도, 소매치기 등 피해가 많이 발생하므로 시내 이동시에는 그랩(Grab) 어플리케이션이나 택시를 이용하시는 것이 바람직합니다. ㅇ 마닐라 공항에서는 안전을 위하여 흰색 일반 택시보다는 그랩(Grab)이나 옐로우(노란색) 택시 또는 선불제 쿠폰 택시를 이용하시고, 택시 승차 후에는 지인에게 차량번호(택시 내부에 기재되어 있음), 승차지점, 목적지 등을 알려주기 바랍니다. [도로교통] ㅇ 한국과 같이 우측통행을 하며, 운전자들이 일반적으로 차선을 잘 지키지 않는 편입니다. 또한 갑작스런 정지 및 차선 변경 등 난폭운전으로 인해 사고 위험성이 높습니다. 시내 중심가에는 일방 통행이 많습니다. ㅇ 필리핀에서는 국제운전면허증을 도착일로부터 90일 동안 사용 가능합니다. 국제운전면허증과 함께 한국 운전면허증 및 여권을 반드시 함께 소지하셔야 하며, 교통 단속원들이 국제운전면허증을 잘 이해하지 못하여 시비가 되는 경우도 간혹 있습니다 - 장기체류자의 경우에는 한국운전면허증을 필리핀 운전면허증으로 교환하여 사용하는 것이 편리합니다. - 여권 및 운전면허증 원본을 가지고 주필리핀 한국대사관에서 공증을 받아 육상교통국(LTO)에 제출한 후 제반 절차를 거치면 면허증을 발급받을 수 있습니다. ㅇ 무보험 차량이 많고 사고가 발생해도 무책임하므로 접촉사고가 나지 않도록 주의해야 합니다. ㅇ 필리핀의 가장 흔한 대중교통 수단은 지프니이지만, 여행객의 경우 안전을 위해 택시를 이용하는 것이 좋습니다. 또한 택시의 경우에도 급한 경우가 아니라면 호텔이 직접 운영하는 택시나 호텔을 통해 부른 택시를 이용하는 것이 안전합니다. - 그랩(Grab) App: 동남아권 국가에서 주로 사용하는 차량공유 서비스를 제공하는 스마트폰 어플리케이션으로 출발지와 목적지를 설정하여 탑승이 가능하고 이동거리, 시간대 등에 따라 고정된 요금을 지불하기 때문에 보다 안전합니다. - 지프니 : 필리핀 서민의 기본적인 교통수단으로 군용지프 모양을 한 승합차로 통상 15-20명 이상 승차합니다. 지프니 승객을 대상으로 한 강도 등 강력 사건이 발생하므로 가급적 이용하지 않는 것이 좋습니다. - 트라이씨클 : 모터싸이클을 개조해서 승객 3-5명을 태울 수 있게 만든 대중 교통수단으로 보통 짧은 노선을 운행합니다. - 택시 : 기본요금은 30~40페소로 지역에 따라 다르며, 합승은 극히 드물고, 택시를 이용할 때는 미터기 작동을 확인한 후 요금을 지불하도록 하되, 미리 택시 요금을 흥정한 뒤 타기도 합니다. - 프리미엄 택시 : 공항, 호텔 및 쇼핑몰 등에서 주로 운영되는 택시로 미터기 요금 이외에 30~50페소가 추가됩니다. - 버스 : 주로 장거리 시외 지역을 운행하며 거리에 따라 요금이 다르나 에어컨의 버스의 기본 요금은 11페소, 일반 버스는 8페소입니다. - 메트로 마닐라내 지상 경전철(MRT, LRT) : 구간별 요금이 다르나 기본 요금이 15페소 정도이고, 오전 5시부터 오후 10시30분(LRT는 새벽1시)까지 이용 가능합니다. ※ 비용은 여행시점에 별도 확인 필요

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생을 대비해 현지어를 구사하는 운전기사를 고용하는 것이 유리 ㅇ 택시와 같은 대중교통 탑승시 가급적 호텔, 대형쇼핑몰, 기타 경비원이 차량번호를 기록하는 곳에서 승차-은행, 환전소, 현금인출기 앞에서 승차 삼가

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 필리핀은 열대성 기후 지역으로서, 6월-10월은 우기이고, 11월-3월은 건기입니다. 4월-5월은 연중 가장 더운 날씨를 보입니다. ㅇ 일자별 기상 정보는 필리핀 기상청 홈페이지(www.pagasa.dost.gov.ph)에서 알아볼 수 있습니다.

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: ㅇ 시차는 한국 대비, 1시간이 늦습니다. ㅇ 필리핀 국내에서는 보통 휴대폰을 사용합니다. 보통 전화기를 사서 Sim에 일정 금액을 충전(load)하면, 그 금액만큼 사용합니다. ㅇ 한국으로의 국제전화는 00+82+2(서울)+한국전화번호로 하면 됩니다. ㅇ 공중전화는 사용이 거의 불가능합니다.

themes.heading: Latest update
themes.text: Still current at:24 July 2026 Updated:12 May 2026 Latest update:Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere in the Philippines. Possible targets include airports, public transport, hotels, shopping malls, restaurants, major events, places of worship and tourist areas. The risk of terrorism linked to the situation in the Middle East may escalate worldwide. There's growing concern about increased risk of attacks on Israeli and US interests globally and on Jewish communities, including in the Philippines. We continue to advise reconsider your need to travel to eastern Mindanao (excluding Camiguin, Dinagat and Siargao Islands) and do not travel to other areas of Mindanao due to the very high threat of terrorism (see 'Safety'). Avoid all protests, demonstrations or rallies. Check local media for the latest information. Public protests and large gatherings occur and may turn violent. It's against the law for foreigners to join public demonstrations. You may be arrested and deported if you participate. To reduce your risk of being scammed, robbed or harmed, only use official rideshare providers accredited by the Philippines Government or hire cars booked through hotels, including for travel to and from airports (see 'Safety'). Air pollution can reach unhealthy levels in the Philippines, particularly in Manila, and may cause breathing problems (see ‘Health’). We advise: Exercise a high degree of caution in the Philippines overall due to the threat of terrorism and violent crime. Higher levels apply in some areas. Download Philippines Download Asia

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: It's illegal for foreigners to join public demonstrations. You may be arrested and deported if you participate. Avoid protests and large gatherings as they may turn violent without warning. Protests can result in disruptions to transport and other essential services. The risk of terrorism linked to the situation in the Middle East may escalate in the region and worldwide. There's growing concern about increased risk of attacks on Israeli and US interests globally and on Jewish communities, including in the Philippines. The US Embassy in Manila has advised Americans in Manila to remain alert to their surroundings at all times, especially in public places popular with foreigners or Americans, and to avoid facilities such as the Chabad Jewish Center and The Jewish Association of the Philippines. Monitor US security alerts in your location and follow this advice. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere in the Philippines, including at protests. Possible targets include aviation and airports, public transport, hotels, shopping malls, restaurants, major events, places of worship and tourist areas. Be alert to possible threats. Take official warnings seriously and follow advice of local authorities. On 3 December 2023 there was a terrorist attack in Marawi City resulting in four dead and many injured, leading to heightened security arrangements in Mindanao and elsewhere, including Manila. Do not travel to central and western Mindanao, including the Zamboanga Peninsula, Sulu Archipelago and the southern Sulu Sea area due to the very high threat of terrorism and kidnapping. Reconsider your need to travel to eastern Mindanao (excluding Camiguin, Dinagat and Siargao Islands) due to political violence and terrorism threat. Kidnappings occur in the Philippines and may target popular tourist areas. The threat of kidnapping and piracy in the southern Philippines is high, especially in the area of the Sulu Archipelago. If you travel to the southern Philippines despite our advice, get professional security advice. Ensure your accommodation has proper security measures. Don't travel by boat. Violent crimes, such as armed robbery in public places, sexual assault, and murder, occur regularly and are a serious problem in the Philippines, including Metro Manila. Many crimes involve guns. Gangs often drug tourists before robbing or assaulting them. Pickpocketing, bag snatching, and scams are common. Only use ATMs in secure locations. Stay alert and be careful in crowded shopping malls and other public places. Avoid using public transport. Use accredited rideshare providers, or hotel hire cars, especially at airports. Commonly used providers include Grab (Mytaxi.Ph Inc), inDrive, and JoyRide. You should check ride share providers are approved through the Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) website by entering the company name in the search column. Its been reported that accreditations of some providers were cancelled. Drink spiking can occur, including in areas frequented by foreigners in Metro Manila. See our advice on partying safely while overseas. Extreme weather can impact the Philippines. Heavy rain, dangerous winds occur, causing flash flooding and landslides. Transport, including airport closures and flight cancellations, along with essential services, may be affected. If you're travelling, contact your airline or travel provider for updates. Know your accommodation's evacuation plan. Monitor PAGASA for the latest advisories. Typhoons, tropical storms, flooding, landslides, earthquakes, volcanic eruptions and tsunamis are common. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Medical facilities are generally adequate in major cities. Medical facilities are limited outside cities. Malaria and dengue are common, especially in many provincial areas. There's been an increase in cases of dengue in Metro Manila. Other insect-borne diseases include Japanese encephalitis and filariasis. Consider taking anti-malarial medication. Foodborne, waterborne and other infectious diseases include cholera, typhoid and hepatitis. Drink only boiled or bottled water. Avoid raw or undercooked food. Don't swim in lakes or rivers. HIV/AIDS is a risk. Take precautions if you engage in high-risk activities. Air pollution can reach unhealthy levels in the Philippines, particularly in Manila. It may cause breathing problems. If you have a pre-existing medical condition, particularly a heart or lung condition, you may be especially affected. Discuss your travel plans with a doctor. Children, pregnant women and the elderly should also take extra care. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: If you're accused of a crime, authorities may not let you leave the Philippines until investigations and legal proceedings are finished. These can take a long time, sometimes years. Don't use or carry illegal drugs. Penalties are severe and police are strictly enforcing drug laws. Mandatory sentences are common for possessing small amounts of drugs and may include life imprisonment. The Philippines has strong laws against child sex crimes and human trafficking. Sentences can include life imprisonment. You can also be charged under Australian law for child sex crimes committed overseas. Local laws don't protect LGBTQIA+ rights. Consider avoiding public displays of affection. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: You can enter the Philippines without a visa for a stay period of 30 days or less, provided that you meet all of the requirements and certain conditions. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. You should contact the nearest embassy or consulate of the Philippines for the latest details. You must register your travel to and from the Philippines on the eTravel Card online portal in order to receive a QR code to use for arrival and departure formalities. Airlines and countries you transit through may have different requirements. Check and fully understand the requirements of the airline you're travelling with. Also check the requirements of any country you're transiting through. Transit passengers should ensure they meet all entry requirements for the Philippines. If you arrive at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA) and need to change terminals, you can use the airport's free shuttle bus or an accredited rideshare provider such as Grab. If you're traveling on an international flight, allow enough time to clear immigration and complete all required checks for your connecting flight. Check with your airline about whether you need to collect and re-check-in your luggage between connecting flights. Failure to meet entry requirements may result in you being denied entry to the Philippines and detained until the next available flight to your port of departure or Australia, at your own expense. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. For consular help, contact the Australian Embassy in Manila. To stay up to date with local information, follow the Embassy’s social media accounts Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorist attacks, including bombings, are possible anytime and anywhere in the Philippines, including Manila. The Philippine media occasionally reports that terrorists may be planning attacks with little or no warning. Possible targets for future attacks include places travellers and expats visit, such as: aviation and airports transport hubs and the metro system hotels, shopping malls, clubs, restaurants, bars and markets places of worship outdoor recreation events other tourist areas To stay safe: report suspicious activities or items to the police check the media for new threats take official warnings seriously avoid protests and demonstrations follow the instructions of local authorities If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities.

themes.heading: Mindanao and the southern Philippines
themes.text: These areas have a high threat of: terrorist attacks kidnapping piracy violent crime violent clashes between armed groups We continue to advise do not travel to central or western Mindanao, and reconsider your need to travel to eastern Mindanao. If despite our advice you visit these areas: get professional security advice have effective personal security measures in place If you get into trouble, the Australian Government may be unable to help. In most cases, our ability to provide consular assistance in reconsider your need to travel and do not travel locations is extremely limited. Any travel by Australian officials to do not travel locations is subject to high-level approval and is based on a rigorous risk assessment.

themes.heading: Terror attacks
themes.text: Terror attacks include: On 3 December 2023 there was a terrorist attack in Marawi City resulting in four dead and many injured, leading to heightened security arrangements in Mindanao and elsewhere, including Manila. On 24 August 2020, a dual bomb attack in Jolo, Sulu killed 14 people and injured 75. Terrorism is a threat worldwide. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping can happen anywhere, even in places that seem safe. Foreigners, including Australians, have been kidnapped overseas whilst travelling. Kidnapping can happen anywhere in the Philippines, in rural, urban and coastal areas. Kidnappers in the Philippines have targeted foreign nationals. Kidnappers in the Philippines have killed victims. Kidnapping can be business, criminal or terrorism-related, and foreign nationals are affected. The threat of terrorism-related kidnapping is highest in central and western Mindanao, especially: along the Zamboanga Peninsula in the Sulu Archipelago off the coast of Sabah, Malaysia Locals, expatriates and foreigners, including Australians, have been kidnapped in central and western Mindanao and in eastern Malaysia. Kidnappers can hold their victims for a long time, even for years, before releasing them. Kidnappers have sometimes killed their captives. Criminals in coastal areas have targeted individuals on private vessels, as well as crews on cargo vessels. The threat of piracy in the southern Philippines is high, especially in the Sulu archipelago. You can find more information on reducing the risk of piracy here: Reducing the risk of piracy. You’re responsible for your own safety. You can find more information on how to reduce your risk of kidnapping here: Kidnapping. The Australian Government's longstanding policy is that it doesn't make payments or concessions to kidnappers. Ransom payments to kidnappers have funded further terrorist attacks and criminal activity. Ransom payments to terrorist groups are likely to contravene Australian counter-terrorism financing laws.

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Avoid all protests, demonstrations, or rallies, and monitor local media for updated information. See our general information on staying safe in case of demonstrations. Public protests and events that draw large groups of people can occur and can gather quickly. Sometimes they can turn violent. It's illegal to take part in political rallies if you're not Filipino. If you're involved, authorities may deport Australians or cancel their visas. Armed clashes occur between security forces and militants throughout the country, but especially in central and western Mindanao. The armed wing of the Communist Party of the Philippines (CPP), the New People’s Army (CPP-NPA), operates throughout the Philippines. They are often involved in attacks targeting Philippine security forces. In central and western Mindanao, armed clashes between rival clans and other armed groups are common, often resulting in deaths. If you see a confrontation between police and criminals or between rival groups, leave the area straight away. More information: Protests and civil unrest

themes.heading: Violent crime
themes.text: Violent crimes, such as armed robbery in public places, sexual assault, and murder, occur regularly and are a serious problem in the Philippines, including Metro Manila. Gun ownership is widespread and poorly regulated. This adds to the high rate of violent crime. Gunfights between police and criminals have occurred in the past, including in tourist areas in Manila. People, including bystanders, have been killed or injured in such incidents. Terrorist groups are sometimes involved in violent criminal activities in rural areas. They target business interests, including mining projects, for extortion. Several Australians have died after property disputes turned violent. There have been instances where armed criminals have robbed and killed or injured people on: buses the metro jeepneys Avoid travel on these forms of transport. Gangs are active in Manila, and provincial resort towns. Gangs can drug, rob or assault tourists.

themes.heading: Petty crime and scams
themes.text: Pickpocketing and bag snatching are common, including in Manila. Be careful in crowded shopping malls, bars and nightclubs, and other public places. Scams, including those involving property ownership, business ventures, credit cards and ATMs, are common. Seek legal advice before committing to any major financial decision. Online relationship and friendship scams are common. In the past, victims, which have included Australians, have been blackmailed or found themselves facing legal action. Be cautious of anyone you meet online from overseas. Learn the signs of a relationship scam. Know what to watch for. Never send money. If you've already given them money, don't go overseas to try to get it back. Always confirm their identity (including their age), before going overseas to meet them. Learn more about internet friendship, dating and marriage scams on ScamWatch. To protect yourself from scams: undertake due diligence when entering into business ventures only use ATMs in secure locations such as banks, shops and shopping centres always keep your debit and credit cards in sight To protect yourself from crime: always keep your personal belongings close, especially in crowded areas monitor local sources for information about new security risks

themes.heading: Tours and adventure activities
themes.text: Transport and tour operators don't always follow safety and maintenance standards. This includes for adventure activities such as diving. If you plan to do an adventure activity: check if your travel insurance policy covers it ask about and insist on minimum safety requirements always use available safety gear, such as life jackets or seatbelts If proper safety equipment isn't available, use another provider.

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: Tropical cyclones and typhoons impact the Philippines. Heavy rain and dangerous winds can occur, causing flash flooding and landslides. Transport, including airport closures and flight cancellations, along with essential services, can be affected in severe weather. If you’re travelling, contact your airline or travel provider. If you're in affected areas, check the Philippine Atmospheric Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) for the latest updates and follow instructions of local authorities. Know your accommodation's evacuation plan. The Philippines experiences frequent natural disasters and severe weather, including: Typhoons Volcanic eruptions Earthquakes Tsunamis To protect yourself during a natural disaster: secure your passport in a safe, waterproof place monitor news and other weather information sources such as the Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System take official warnings seriously and follow the advice of local authorities have a plan of what to do keep in touch with friends and family After a natural disaster, identify local sources of support and advice that can help you. Contact your tour operator to check if services are affected.

themes.heading: Earthquakes and volcanoes
themes.text: The Philippines is located on the Ring of Fire. Earthquakes and volcanic eruptions are common. Following an earthquake or volcanic eruption, there may be disruptions to essential services. Check media reports and follow the advice of the local authorities. Volcanic activity may increase with little or no notice. It may disrupt flights in the region. Individual airlines make their own decisions about flight operations. Contact your airline or tour operator for up-to-date information. If there is major volcanic activity, monitor warnings from the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS). PHIVOLCS has set up permanent danger zones (PDZ) around the summits of several volcanoes: Kanlaon volcano in Negros Occidental and Negros Oriental Provinces has a 4km PDZ Mayon volcano in Albay Province has a 6km PDZ Bulusan volcano in Sorsogon Province has a 4km PDZ the entire volcanic island of Taal is a PDZ Avoid areas around all of these volcanoes. The eruption of Mayon Volcano on 12 January 2026 resulted in several domestic flight cancellations within the region. On 12 January 2020, Taal Volcano, in the southern Luzon province of Batangas, erupted. Residents within 14 km were advised to evacuate, and ash clouds travelling over Manila affected both international and domestic flights at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA).

themes.heading: Tsunamis
themes.text: Large and destructive tsunamis can happen. If there's a tsunami warning: check the US Tsunami Warning Centre website regularly follow the advice of local authorities and evacuation orders know the emergency plans at your accommodation don't travel by sea if it's not safe to do so Move to high ground straight away if: you feel a strong earthquake that makes it hard to stand up you feel a weak, rolling earthquake that lasts a minute or more you see a sudden rise or fall in sea level you hear loud and unusual noises from the sea Don't wait for official warnings. Once on high ground, check local media and weather services.

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance If you travel to central and western Mindanao despite our advice, you'll probably need a specialised insurance policy that covers travel to high-risk destinations. Most Australian policies won't cover you for travel to these areas.

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas If you need to speak to counselling services in the Philippines, contact the following: In Touch (Crisis Line # +63 2 8893 7603 | +63 917 800 1123 | +63 922 893 8944) National Centre for Mental Health (NCMH) (Crisis Hotline +63 917 899 8272 | +63917 898 8272) You can also contact the Australian Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305.

themes.heading: Insect-borne illnesses
themes.text: Outbreaks of insect-borne illnesses are increasing in Manila and neighbouring provinces, including Dengue Malaria Japanese encephalitis Filariasis or Elephantiasis These illnesses are more common during the rainy season between May and December. If you're pregnant: discuss travel plans with your doctor consider deferring non-essential travel to affected areas To protect yourself from disease: make sure your accommodation is insect-proof use insect repellent wear long, loose, light-coloured clothing consider taking medicine to prevent malaria Ask your doctor about health risks and vaccines before you go. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: HIV/AIDS
themes.text: HIV/AIDS is a risk for travellers. Take precautions if you engage in activities that expose you to risk of infection. Condoms may be difficult to find outside Manila.

themes.heading: Other health risks
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne, parasitic, and other diseases are common, including: Cholera Typhoid Hepatitis A, B and C Tuberculosis Meningitis COVID-19 Bilharzia (schistosomiasis) Leptospirosis Rabies Measles and Rubella - in February 2026, the Philippines Department of Health warned about a possible outbreak of measles and rubella in Mindanao if the region fails to reach herd immunity against the diseases. Poliomyelitis (polio) Ciguatera fish poisoning Swimmers in coastal resort areas have also reported illness from pollution. To reduce your risk of illness: drink boiled water or water from bottles with sealed lids avoid ice cubes avoid uncooked and undercooked food, such as salads don't swim in fresh water sources always check with local authorities before swimming in waters avoid direct contact with dogs don't feed or pat animals avoid contact with other animals, including bats if bitten or scratched by an animal, immediately use soap and water to wash the wound thoroughly and seek urgent medical attention. Get medical advice if you suspect food poisoning or have a fever or diarrhoea. More information: Infectious diseases Philippines Department of Health Australian Department of Health and Aged Care

themes.heading: Bird flu (avian influenza)
themes.text: Avian influenza occurs in farm birds. To protect yourself from avian influenza: stay away from live poultry don't visit live bird and animal markets, including 'wet' markets and poultry farms When preparing food, handle poultry properly. Make sure you thoroughly cook all parts of the poultry.

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: There’s no agreement between Australia and the Philippines for free healthcare. Tourists must pay for all medical care, even if they need to stay in hospital or be evacuated. This can be very expensive. Make sure your travel insurance covers you. The Australian Government won't pay for these costs. Major private hospitals, particularly in Metro Manila, are well-equipped and internationally accredited. Medical facilities in rural/regional areas may have limited services and facilities, including in some popular tourist destinations. Most hospitals need an up-front payment or a guarantee of payment before they'll treat you. Treatment can be expensive. Hospitals may cease treatment if ongoing bills are not settled. You should ensure you understand what your Philippine health fund or travel insurance provider will and won't cover. Decompression chambers are available in: Bacolod, Negros Occidental Batangas City Cavite City Cebu City, Cebu Coron, Palawan Davao City, Davao Makati City Manadaue City, Cebu Quezon City Panglao, Bohol Puerto Galera, Oriental Mindoro Puerto Princesca City, Palawan Tacloban City, Leyte It is common for decompression chambers not to be working. If you become seriously ill or injured, you may need to be evacuated. Medical evacuation can be very expensive.

themes.heading: Air pollution
themes.text: There can be moderate to high levels of air pollution. Pollution can increase the risk of breathing problems. Children, pregnant women, the elderly and those with pre-existing heart and lung conditions may be especially affected. If you're concerned about the levels of air pollution: seek medical advice follow advice from local authorities about methods to reduce exposure monitor an air quality index reduce your exposure More information: Philippines Government Ambient Air Quality Monitoring website Air Quality index for the Philippines Air quality (Australian Centre for Disease Control) Air pollution (WHO)

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug offences are severe. They include long jail terms, including life imprisonment, for even small amounts. A major clampdown on drugs - the war on drugs - has been underway since 2016. There are mandatory jail sentences for possessing even small amounts of illicit drugs. Thousands have been arrested and a large number of people have been killed in drug-related violence. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Local customs
themes.text: Local laws don't protect LGBTQIA+ rights. Locals generally tolerate the LGBTQIA+ community. However, open displays of affection between same-sex partners may offend. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visas
themes.text: You are permitted to enter the Philippines without a visa for a stay period of 30 days or less provided that you meet all of the requirements. Failure to meet entry requirements may result in you being denied entry to the Philippines and detained until the next available flight to your port of departure or Australia, at your own expense. See the following links for the latest information: Philippine Embassy in Australia website Philippine Bureau of Immigration website Philippine Tourism Ensure you keep your visa up to date. Failure to do so may result in you being: fined or arrested detained in immigration detention deported back to Australia at your cost blacklisted from future travel to the Philippines Detention conditions in the Philippines do not compare with Australian standards, and deportation processes can be lengthy. You can apply to extend your tourist visa (visa waiver) either online or in person through the Philippine Bureau of Immigration and pay the appropriate fees. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Get further details about visas, currency, customs and quarantine rules from: the nearest embassy or consulate of the Philippines the Philippine Bureau of Immigration website

themes.heading: Border measures
themes.text: Australians are permitted to enter the Philippines without a visa for a period of 30 days or less provided that you meet all of the requirements. Your passport must be valid for at least six months, and you must have onward/return tickets for no later than 30 days from your arrival in the Philippines. You must register at the eTravel online portal and complete the electronic health declaration checklist in order to receive a QR code for validation by the Bureau of Quarantine. Children under 15 years of age may need a Waiver of Exclusion Ground if they're: not accompanied by a parent coming to meet a parent or parents in the Philippines You should declare any: local cash of more than PHP50,000 to Bureau of Customs officers at the airport when you arrive or leave amounts of more than $US10,000 or of the same value in a different foreign currency this covers all forms of currency, not only cash. Confirm requirements through your travel provider or nearest Philippine Embassy. Check and fully understand the requirements of the airline you're travelling with, as their requirements may be different. Failure to meet entry requirements may result in you being denied entry to the Philippines and detained until the next available flight to your port of departure or to Australia. This will be at your own expense.

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate. If you lose your passport on arrival into the Philippines, before passing through Immigration, you'll be refused entry to the Philippines. You'll be sent back to your port of origin by the Philippine Government and the Australian Government will be unable to help.

themes.heading: Passport with ‘X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Philippine peso (PHP). You can easily convert AUD for PHP at airports, banks, shopping centres, tourist locations, in major cities and towns. ATMs are widely available. International credit cards are generally accepted in hotels, restaurants and higher-end shops, particularly in cities and larger towns. However, it is advisable to keep some cash. Card skimming occurs.

themes.heading: Driving permit
themes.text: You can use a current Australian driver's licence (digital driver's licences may not be accepted) for up to 90 days after you arrive. After that, you need to get a Philippines licence. More information: Land Transportation Office

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Roads are generally congested and often in poor condition. Drivers often break the road rules. Avoid driving off the national highways and other sealed roads, especially at night. You're more likely to die in a motor vehicle accident in the Philippines than in Australia. More information: Driving or riding

themes.heading: Motorcycles
themes.text: Certified helmets are mandatory when riding in major cities. Accidents are frequent. Check if your travel insurance policy covers you when riding a motorbike and if any exclusions apply. You might not be covered if you don't have a valid Australian motorcycle licence.

themes.heading: Taxis
themes.text: Don't use taxis. There have been cases where drivers and their accomplices have robbed or harmed passengers. Use government-approved rideshare providers or hire cars sourced from hotels as a preference, especially at airports where taxi scam gangs may operate. More information: Taxi scams

themes.heading: Public transport
themes.text: Public road (bus, jeepneys) and rail transport is widely used throughout the country but should be avoided due to safety and security risks (see 'Safety'). More information: Transport and getting around safely

themes.heading: Sea travel
themes.text: Sea travel in the Philippines can be hazardous. Avoid ferries if you can. If you must travel by sea: use a ferry service with a good reputation don't use ferries that are overcrowded and appear poorly maintained take your own lifejacket Piracy and kidnapping occur in Philippine waters (see 'Safety'). More information: Travelling by boat

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Check the Philippines' air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network website. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas.

themes.heading: Australian Embassy, Manila
themes.text: Level 23 Tower 2 RCBC Plaza 6819 Ayala Avenue Makati City, 1200, Philippines Phone: (+63 2) 7757 8100 Fax: (+63 2) 7757 8344 Email Consular assistance: Consular.Manila@dfat.gov.au Email Passport enquiries: MNLA.Passports@dfat.gov.au Email Notarial enquiries: Notarials.Manila@dfat.gov.au Website: philippines.embassy.gov.au Facebook: Australia in the Philippines X: @AusAmbPH Check the Embassy website for details about opening hours and any temporary closures.

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia
